county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1861 from Mackinac and Schoolcraft in the central Upper Peninsula. County seat is Escanaba. Named for the delta shape of the county at the mouth of rivers into Little Bay de Noc.
county_metadata.research_tips: Delta County Register of Deeds in Escanaba holds land records from 1861 when the county was organized from Mackinac and Schoolcraft. County Clerk maintains marriage records and court files. The Delta County Historical Society and Archives has local genealogical materials. Iron smelting, lumber, and fishing drove early settlement. Scandinavian (Swedish, Norwegian) and French-Canadian immigrants formed significant communities. Escanaba was an important iron ore shipping port. Church records from Lutheran, Catholic, and Methodist congregations are essential. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records. Check Mackinac and Schoolcraft counties for pre-1861 records. Archives of Michigan has UP industrial records.

source_profiles.source_name: Delta County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1861 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

source_profiles.source_name: Delta County Probate Records -- Probate Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, guardianship records, and administration files from 1861. Michigan Probate Courts also handled adoption records, mental health commitments, and name changes.

source_profiles.source_name: Delta County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1861. Michigan County Clerks issue marriage licenses. State registration of marriages began 1867. Pre-1867 marriages may also be documented in church records.

source_profiles.source_name: Delta County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census for Delta County from 1870 to 1950. Michigan territorial censuses (1827, 1834) may include early residents. Michigan state censuses (1874, 1884, 1894, 1904) provide intercensal data.

source_profiles.source_name: Delta County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Michigan is a public land state. Original federal land patents for Delta County are available free online at BLM GLO. Cash entry, homestead, and military bounty land warrants. Essential for identifying first landowners.

source_profiles.repository: Find A Grave / Local historical and genealogical societies
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Delta County churches, township, and family burial grounds. Find A Grave has growing online coverage. Local historical and genealogical societies have published cemetery surveys. Michigan Cemetery Source at the Library of Michigan is a statewide index.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1891 from Iron, Marquette, and Menominee in the Upper Peninsula. County seat is Iron Mountain. Named for Don M. Dickinson, US Postmaster General. Iron mining county.
county_metadata.research_tips: Dickinson County Register of Deeds in Iron Mountain holds land records from 1891 organization. County Clerk has marriage records and court files. The Dickinson County Library has a genealogy collection. Iron mining was the primary industry -- Chapin Mine, Millie Mine, and other operations employed thousands. Company town records and mining employment rolls at Michigan Technological University Archives. Italian, Cornish, Finnish, and Swedish immigrants predominated. Check Iron, Marquette, and Menominee counties for pre-1891 records. Catholic and Lutheran church records document immigrant families. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records. Iron Mountain Daily News archives.

source_profiles.source_name: Dickinson County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1891 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

source_profiles.source_name: Dickinson County Probate Records -- Probate Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, guardianship records, and administration files from 1891. Michigan Probate Courts also handled adoption records, mental health commitments, and name changes.

source_profiles.source_name: Dickinson County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1891. Michigan County Clerks issue marriage licenses. State registration of marriages began 1867. Pre-1867 marriages may also be documented in church records.

source_profiles.source_name: Dickinson County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census for Dickinson County from 1900 to 1950. Michigan territorial censuses (1827, 1834) may include early residents. Michigan state censuses (1874, 1884, 1894, 1904) provide intercensal data.

source_profiles.source_name: Dickinson County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Michigan is a public land state. Original federal land patents for Dickinson County are available free online at BLM GLO. Cash entry, homestead, and military bounty land warrants. Essential for identifying first landowners.

source_profiles.source_name: Dickinson County Mining Company Records
source_profiles.repository: Michigan Technological University Archives / Archives of Michigan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Employment rolls, payroll records, accident reports, and company town records from iron mining operations in Dickinson County. Michigan Tech's Copper Country Historical Collections and the Archives of Michigan hold mining company records. Essential for tracing immigrant mining families.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1837 from unorganized territory at Michigan statehood. County seat is Charlotte. Named for John H. Eaton, US Secretary of War. Agricultural county adjacent to Lansing.
county_metadata.research_tips: Eaton County Register of Deeds in Charlotte holds land records from 1837 when the county was organized at statehood. County Clerk maintains marriage licenses from 1837. Probate Court has estate files. The Eaton County Historical Commission and local genealogical society have family files and cemetery transcriptions. Early settlers came from New York and New England. Eaton County is adjacent to Ingham (Lansing) and grew as a suburban and agricultural area. Strong Methodist, Baptist, and Congregational church records. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records. BLM GLO land patents. The Archives of Michigan in Lansing is conveniently located for Eaton County research.

source_profiles.source_name: Eaton County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1837 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, guardianship records, and administration files from 1837. Michigan Probate Courts also handled adoption records, mental health commitments, and name changes.

source_profiles.source_name: Eaton County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1837. Michigan County Clerks issue marriage licenses. State registration of marriages began 1867. Pre-1867 marriages may also be documented in church records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census for Eaton County from 1840 to 1950. Michigan territorial censuses (1827, 1834) may include early residents. Michigan state censuses (1874, 1884, 1894, 1904) provide intercensal data.

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Michigan is a public land state. Original federal land patents for Eaton County are available free online at BLM GLO. Cash entry, homestead, and military bounty land warrants. Essential for identifying first landowners.

source_profiles.source_name: Eaton County Tax Rolls -- Archives of Michigan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Annual tax rolls listing property owners, assessed values, and personal property. Michigan tax rolls from 1837 at the Archives of Michigan. Essential for tracking land ownership and residency between census years.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1853 from Mackinac County in northwest Lower Michigan. County seat is Petoskey. Named for Robert Emmet, Irish patriot. Resort region with Ottawa tribal heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: Emmet County Register of Deeds in Petoskey holds land records from 1853 organization. County Clerk has marriage records and court files. The Little Traverse Historical Society and Museum in Petoskey has genealogical collections including early settler records and resort-era documentation. The Little Traverse Bay Bands of Odawa Indians have tribal enrollment records and the Ziibiwing Center archives. French fur traders and Ottawa families intermarried extensively -- check Catholic parish records (Holy Childhood Church, Harbor Springs). Resort tourism began in the 1870s (Bay View, Petoskey). Ernest Hemingway family connections to Petoskey/Walloon Lake. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records.

source_profiles.source_name: Emmet County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1853 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

source_profiles.source_name: Emmet County Probate Records -- Probate Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, guardianship records, and administration files from 1853. Michigan Probate Courts also handled adoption records, mental health commitments, and name changes.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1853. Michigan County Clerks issue marriage licenses. State registration of marriages began 1867. Pre-1867 marriages may also be documented in church records.

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Michigan is a public land state. Original federal land patents for Emmet County are available free online at BLM GLO. Cash entry, homestead, and military bounty land warrants. Essential for identifying first landowners.

source_profiles.repository: NARA (Great Lakes Agency) / Tribal Government Offices / Archives of Michigan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Treaty records, tribal census rolls (annuity rolls), allotment records, and agency correspondence for Ottawa/Odawa communities in Emmet County. NARA holds Bureau of Indian Affairs records (Great Lakes Agency). Tribal government offices maintain enrollment records. Archives of Michigan has related territorial records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1836 from Lapeer, Shiawassee, and Saginaw in east-central Lower Michigan. County seat is Flint. Named for Genesee County, New York. Automobile manufacturing center.
county_metadata.research_tips: Genesee County Register of Deeds in Flint holds land records from 1836. County Clerk maintains marriage licenses and court records. Probate Court has estate files. The Genesee County Historical Society and the Sloan Museum of Discovery have genealogical collections. Flint became a major automobile manufacturing center -- General Motors employment records are essential for tracing worker families (Buick, Chevrolet, AC Spark Plug). The Flint Public Library has city directories, newspapers, and local history. The Great Migration brought significant African American population from the South. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records. The Archives of Michigan has labor and industrial records. Catholic, Baptist, and AME church records are important for diverse communities.

source_profiles.source_name: Genesee County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1836 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

source_profiles.source_name: Genesee County Probate Records -- Probate Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, guardianship records, and administration files from 1836. Michigan Probate Courts also handled adoption records, mental health commitments, and name changes.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1836. Michigan County Clerks issue marriage licenses. State registration of marriages began 1867. Pre-1867 marriages may also be documented in church records.

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Michigan is a public land state. Original federal land patents for Genesee County are available free online at BLM GLO. Cash entry, homestead, and military bounty land warrants. Essential for identifying first landowners.

source_profiles.source_name: Automobile Industry and Labor Records -- Genesee County
source_profiles.repository: Walter P. Reuther Library (Wayne State University) / Archives of Michigan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Automobile manufacturing employment records, union (UAW) records, and industrial documentation for Genesee County. The Walter P. Reuther Library at Wayne State University holds United Auto Workers records and related labor archives. City directories and employee rosters trace worker families. Essential for 20th-century family research in Michigan's industrial counties.

source_profiles.source_name: Genesee County Tax Rolls -- Archives of Michigan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Annual tax rolls listing property owners, assessed values, and personal property. Michigan tax rolls from 1836 at the Archives of Michigan. Essential for tracking land ownership and residency between census years.

source_profiles.source_name: Genesee County Historical Newspapers
source_profiles.repository: Archives of Michigan / Library of Congress / Newspapers.com
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Historical newspapers for Genesee County including local weeklies and dailies. Obituaries, legal notices, marriage and death announcements, estate settlements. Michigan Digital Newspaper Archive and Chronicling America have digitized collections.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1875 from Midland County in central Lower Michigan. County seat is Gladwin. Named for Major Henry Gladwin, British commandant at Fort Detroit. Lumber and agriculture.
county_metadata.research_tips: Gladwin County Register of Deeds in Gladwin holds land records from 1875 when the county was organized from Midland. County Clerk has marriage records and court files. The Gladwin County Historical Society has local materials. Lumber industry drove early settlement. The county was sparsely populated even after organization. For pre-1875 records, check Midland County. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records. BLM GLO land patents document original settlers. The Archives of Michigan has supplementary territorial-era records. Cedar and hardwood logging replaced the white pine era in Gladwin County.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1875 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Michigan is a public land state. Original federal land patents for Gladwin County are available free online at BLM GLO. Cash entry, homestead, and military bounty land warrants. Essential for identifying first landowners.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Gladwin County churches, township, and family burial grounds. Find A Grave has growing online coverage. county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1887 from Ontonagon County in the western Upper Peninsula. County seat is Bessemer. Named from Ojibwe for 'lake.' Iron mining on the Gogebic Range.
county_metadata.research_tips: Gogebic County Register of Deeds in Bessemer holds land records from 1887 organization. County Clerk has marriage records and court files. The Gogebic County Historical Museum in Ironwood has mining and immigrant community records. Iron mining (Gogebic Iron Range) was the dominant industry. Finnish, Italian, Cornish, and Polish immigrants formed the bulk of the mining workforce. Check Ontonagon County for pre-1887 records. Michigan Technological University Archives has mining company records. Finnish American Heritage Center at Finlandia University in Hancock has Finnish immigrant records. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records. Catholic and Lutheran church records are essential for immigrant families.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1887 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1887. Michigan County Clerks issue marriage licenses. State registration of marriages began 1867. Pre-1867 marriages may also be documented in church records.

source_profiles.repository: Michigan Technological University Archives / Archives of Michigan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Employment rolls, payroll records, accident reports, and company town records from iron mining operations in Gogebic County. Michigan Tech's Copper Country Historical Collections and the Archives of Michigan hold mining company records. Essential for tracing immigrant mining families.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1851 from Omeena (now Manistee) in northwest Lower Michigan. County seat is Traverse City. Named for the Grand Traverse Bay. Cherry capital and resort region.
county_metadata.research_tips: Grand Traverse County Register of Deeds in Traverse City holds land records from 1851. County Clerk maintains marriage licenses and court records. The Grand Traverse Heritage Center and Traverse Area District Library have extensive genealogical collections including the local history room with newspapers, city directories, and family files. The Traverse City State Hospital (Northern Michigan Asylum, opened 1885) records are partially at the Archives of Michigan. Cherry farming heritage -- agricultural census records are valuable. Ojibwe/Ottawa communities along Grand Traverse Bay have records at tribal offices. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records. BLM GLO land patents.

source_profiles.source_name: Grand Traverse County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1851 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Treaty records, tribal census rolls (annuity rolls), allotment records, and agency correspondence for Ottawa/Odawa communities in Grand Traverse County. NARA holds Bureau of Indian Affairs records (Great Lakes Agency). Tribal government offices maintain enrollment records. Archives of Michigan has related territorial records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1855 from unorganized territory in central Lower Michigan. County seat is Ithaca. Named for Colonel Charles Gratiot, US Army engineer. Agricultural county.
county_metadata.research_tips: Gratiot County Register of Deeds in Ithaca holds land records from 1855 organization. County Clerk maintains marriage licenses and court records. Probate Court has estate files. The Gratiot County Historical and Genealogical Society has family files, cemetery records, and local history materials. The county is in the central Lower Peninsula agricultural belt. German and Irish immigrants settled alongside New York and Ohio migrants. St. Louis and Alma are the other principal towns. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records. BLM GLO land patents. Archives of Michigan has supplementary records. Alma College archives hold some local history materials.

source_profiles.source_name: Gratiot County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1855 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1835 from Lenawee County in south-central Lower Michigan. County seat is Hillsdale. Named for its hilly terrain. Home to Hillsdale College (1844), an early abolitionist institution.
county_metadata.research_tips: Hillsdale County Register of Deeds in Hillsdale holds land records from 1835 organization. County Clerk maintains marriage licenses from 1835 and court records. Probate Court has estate files. The Mitchell Research Center at Hillsdale College has local and regional genealogical collections. Hillsdale College (founded 1844) was an early abolitionist institution and admitted Black students and women from its founding. Underground Railroad activity was significant. The Hillsdale County Historical Society has family files. Quaker and Free Will Baptist records are valuable for early settlers. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records. BLM GLO land patents.

source_profiles.source_name: Hillsdale County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1835 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

source_profiles.source_name: Hillsdale County Probate Records -- Probate Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, guardianship records, and administration files from 1835. Michigan Probate Courts also handled adoption records, mental health commitments, and name changes.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1835. Michigan County Clerks issue marriage licenses. State registration of marriages began 1867. Pre-1867 marriages may also be documented in church records.

source_profiles.source_name: Hillsdale County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census for Hillsdale County from 1840 to 1950. Michigan territorial censuses (1827, 1834) may include early residents. Michigan state censuses (1874, 1884, 1894, 1904) provide intercensal data.

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Michigan is a public land state. Original federal land patents for Hillsdale County are available free online at BLM GLO. Cash entry, homestead, and military bounty land warrants. Essential for identifying first landowners.

source_profiles.source_name: Underground Railroad and African American Records -- Hillsdale County
source_profiles.repository: Archives of Michigan / Burton Historical Collection (Detroit Public Library)
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Michigan was a free state, but the Underground Railroad was active throughout southern Michigan with routes to Canada. Hillsdale County records include freedom seekers' documentation, abolitionist society records, African American church records, and community histories. The Burton Historical Collection and Archives of Michigan hold related materials.

source_profiles.source_name: Hillsdale County Tax Rolls -- Archives of Michigan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Annual tax rolls listing property owners, assessed values, and personal property. Michigan tax rolls from 1835 at the Archives of Michigan. Essential for tracking land ownership and residency between census years.
